data.content:
The build confirms **CS0246** — the generated file is written but never added to compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to `obj/`, but glob-based `<Compile>` items expand at evaluation time (before targets run), so the file is invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to the `Compile` and `FileWrites` item groups inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also register it in `FileWrites` so it gets cleaned properly.

Let me use the `including-generated-files` skill since it's directly relevant.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds. Here's the diagnosis and fix:

**Root cause:** MSBuild globs (implicit `<Compile>` items) expand at **evaluation time**, before any targets run. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file during **execution time**, so the compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` so the generated file is explicitly included in compilation and properly cleaned up by `dotnet clean`.
